steps: 1. Extend GameState.ts: add TITLE, TRAINER_BATTLE_INTRO, BADGE_CEREMONY to GamePhase; add DialogState interface, trainerBattleNpcId, lastPokemonCenter, badgeCeremonyTimer fields to GameState

2. Extend MapTypes.ts: extend NPCDefinition with optional trainer fields (isTrainer, trainerId, party, moneyBase, losRange, preBattleDialog, postBattleDialog, badgeReward); add ScriptZone.autoTrigger boolean
3. Create DialogSystem.ts: DialogPage type, DialogQueue state machine, startDialog/handleConfirm/handleUp/handleDown/isActive/update(dt) methods, name entry sub-mode state tracking (120 lines)

4. Create DialogRenderer.ts: renderDialog(ctx, dialogSystem) drawing bottom-48px box with 2-line text window, blinking cursor, choice list with cursor; renderNameEntry(ctx, system) for uppercase grid keyboard UI (130 lines)

5. Create NPC.ts: NPC class (tileX, tileY, facing, spriteId, defeated, dialogPages); TrainerNPC subclass (party, moneyReward, trainerId, losRange, approachPhase, exclaimFrames, walkPixelOffset, walkDir, walkTarget); checkLOS(playerX, playerY, facing, map) method; update(dt) for walk animation (130 lines)

6. Create Trainer.ts: TrainerDefinition interface (id, name, spriteId, party, moneyBase, preBattleDialog, postBattleDialog, badgeReward, tmReward); GymLeaderDefinition subtype; buildTrainerParty(def, ot) factory (80 lines)

7. Create NPCSystem.ts: manages array of NPC instances per map; loadFromMap(map, state) to instantiate from NPCDefinition[]; update(dt, player, map) to run LOS checks; handleInteraction(player) for A-press; checkScriptZones(player) returns scriptId or null (120 lines)

8. Create OnboardingSystem.ts: isNewGame check, TITLE phase render (canvas text + blinking PRESS ENTER), orchestrate OAK_INTRO → nameEntry → rivalNameEntry → fade-in → OVERWORLD; set flags NEW_GAME_STARTED, playerName, rivalName (100 lines)

9. Create pallet-town-scripts.ts: export script registry (PALLET_SCRIPTS: Record<string, DialogPage[]>); OAK_INTRO (3 pages), MOM_DIALOG, STARTER_TABLE_TRIGGER (choice menu), STARTER_OBTAINED sequence, RIVAL_COUNTER mapping, NURSE_JOY_HEAL with YES/NO choice (130 lines)

10. Create viridian-city.json: 30x30 map, tile layout with path/buildings/Pokemart/Pokemon Center, 2 trainer NPCs (Youngster+Lass with Lv3-5 Pokemon), warps S->route-1 and N->route-2 (blocked dialog), NPCs with trainer fields, music: viridian
11. Create pokemon-center.json: 15x10 interior, Nurse Joy NPC at (7,3), PC terminal scriptZone at (12,2), warp exit to viridian-city, scriptZone for NURSE_JOY_HEAL at counter, music: pokemoncenter

12. Create pewter-gym.json: 15x15 interior, checkerboard floor, Youngster trainer NPC blocking path with Geodude Lv10, Brock at (7,2) with Geodude Lv12+Onix Lv14, badgeReward=BOULDER, warp exit, music: gym

13. Update OverworldSystem.ts: inject NPCSystem; add checkNPCInteraction(player) on A-press; integrate NPCSystem.update() result to return TRAINER_BATTLE or DIALOG or SCRIPT_ZONE result types; add NPC as collision obstacle; check SCRIPT_ZONE intersections after move (add ~30 lines keeping total ≤160)

14. Create update to GameEngine.ts: add new map cache entries (viridian-city, pokemon-center, pewter-gym); add NPCSystem as member; route TRAINER_BATTLE_INTRO phase (exclaim+walk→battle); BADGE_CEREMONY phase (badgeZoom animation, addItem TM34, set badge flag); DIALOG phase delegates to DialogSystem+input; TITLE phase delegates to OnboardingSystem; blackout logic when all party fainted; lastPokemonCenter tracking on pokemon-center map entry (add extractable logic to OnboardingSystem and TrainerBattleFlow helpers to keep GameEngine delta ≤50 lines)

15. Update OverworldRenderer.ts: accept NPCSystem as optional param; drawNPCs() uses NPC instances instead of map.npcs; render exclamation bubble (yellow '!' 8px above NPC head) when approachPhase=EXCLAIM; render defeated trainer facing away (change facing direction in sprite draw) (≤160 lines total)

16. Update pallet-town.json: fix warp targetTileX→targetX, targetTileY→targetY (existing bug); add scriptZones array: MOM_DIALOG_ZONE at (7,17), STARTER_TABLE_TRIGGER at (6-8, 3-5); update OAK npc dialog to point to OAK_INTRO scriptId
17. Write dialog-system.test.ts: text crawl speed (SLOW/NORMAL/FAST ms/char), handleConfirm() page advance, choice cursor up/down, name entry add/delete/confirm, isActive() lifecycle

18. Write npc-trainer.test.ts: LOS detection (3-tile cone per facing direction), LOS blocked outside range, defeated flag set after battle, buildTrainerParty creates correct species+level, post-defeat dialog returned
expected_tests: dialog-system.test.ts: startDialog queues pages, isActive() returns true
dialog-system.test.ts: update(dt) advances charIndex at NORMAL speed (30ms/char)
dialog-system.test.ts: update(dt) does not advance before ms threshold
dialog-system.test.ts: handleConfirm() advances to next page when current page complete
dialog-system.test.ts: handleConfirm() calls onComplete when last page done
dialog-system.test.ts: choice handleUp/handleDown wraps cursor correctly
dialog-system.test.ts: choice onSelect callback fires on handleConfirm
dialog-system.test.ts: nameEntry: deleteChar removes last character
dialog-system.test.ts: isActive() returns false after all pages consumed
npc-trainer.test.ts: checkLOS returns false when player is behind trainer
npc-trainer.test.ts: checkLOS returns true when player is in 3-tile cone facing south
npc-trainer.test.ts: checkLOS returns false at distance 4 (out of range)
npc-trainer.test.ts: defeated trainer.defeated=true prevents LOS trigger
npc-trainer.test.ts: buildTrainerParty creates PokemonInstance with correct speciesId and level
npc-trainer.test.ts: RIVAL_COUNTER mapping is correct for all 3 starters
risk_areas: GameEngine.ts is already 247 lines (over Iron Law limit) — mitigate by extracting all new logic to OnboardingSystem and keeping GameEngine delta minimal
Trainer walk-toward-player requires pixel offset tracking for NPCs (like Player entity) — plan NPC.walkPixelOffset field and update loop
Name entry grid keyboard must fit in 160x144 canvas — use full-screen overlay mode, not bottom-dialog mode
Map JSON data for 30x30 Viridian City = 2700 tile numbers × 3 layers — generate programmatically with a simple layout algorithm
BattleState.wildPokemon reuse for trainer battles — handle per-party-slot switching in GameEngine after faint events
Badge ceremony timing: badgeZoom is a standalone function in TransitionRenderer, need to drive its progress from engine timer
Existing pallet-town.json warp uses targetTileX (wrong) not targetX (correct per TS type) — fix this in pallet-town.json update step
Script zone callbacks require access to GameState mutations — pallet-town-scripts.ts uses factory pattern (scripts receive GameState ref)
Pokemon Center heal sets all party HP/PP/status to max — must iterate partyPokemon and reset each field correctly
persistence_strategy: GameState.flags Record<string, boolean|number|string> stores all durable state: TRAINER_[id]_DEFEATED=true, playerName='ASH', rivalName='GARY', badges=[true,false,...], lastPokemonCenterMap, lastPokemonCenterX/Y, money. Persisted via existing SaveSystem (localStorage key pokebrowser_save_v1). NPC runtime instances are reconstructed from flags on map load (defeated state restored from TRAINER_[id]_DEFEATED flags).
error_handling_approach: Dialog scripts are pure data arrays — no IO errors. Map loading errors: GameEngine.loadMap() logs console.warn and falls back to current map (existing pattern). BattleSystem already has try/catch at engine level. New NPCSystem.loadFromMap() silently skips malformed NPC entries (log warning). Badge ceremony: if badge index out of range, clamp to 0-7 and log warning. Blackout: if lastPokemonCenter not set, default to pallet-town (7,5).
